metadata.title: Savoury Oat Porridge with Ginger-Garlic Crumbs
metadata.description: This porridge gets a savoury twist, thanks to the addition of an umami-rich soy butter and crispy ginger-garlic yumbits.
notes: Breakfasts are a solo affair at the OTK, with each person left to their own devices.
Feel free to quadruple the amount of said yumbits: they’re so good sprinkled on to eggs, rice and every other food group.
Make it gluten-free by swapping out the soy with tamari or coconut aminos.
metadata.author: Ottolenghi

steps.action: Toss the spring onions in a bowl with 1 tablespoon of oil and a pinch each of salt and black pepper.

output.description: Spring onions tossed with oil, salt, and pepper.

steps.action: Cook half the spring onions in a hot pan until softened and charred.

output.description: Half of the spring onions are cooked until charred.

steps.action: Cook the remaining spring onions in the same manner.

output.description: Remaining spring onions are cooked until charred.

steps.action: Wipe out the pan and cook the ginger and garlic until deeply golden and crispy.

output.description: Ginger and garlic cooked until golden and crispy.

steps.action: Transfer two-thirds of the ginger-garlic mixture to a small bowl.

output.description: Two-thirds of the ginger-garlic mixture reserved for topping.

steps.action: Add oats, water, salt, and pepper to the ginger-garlic pan and cook until porridge consistency.

output.description: Oats cooked to a loose porridge consistency.

steps.action: Simmer soy sauce and whisk in butter until emulsified.

output.description: Soy sauce and butter combined into a smooth mixture.

steps.action: Divide porridge into bowls and top with soy butter, spring onions, and halved eggs.

output.description: Porridge topped with soy butter, spring onions, and eggs.
